context.real_world_incident: WordPress plugin stored XSS vulnerabilities affected 4M+ sites in 2023, attackers injected malicious scripts in blog comments and user profiles

context.attack_vector: Stored XSS in rich text editor content bypassing HTML sanitization

conversations.value: I'm building a blog platform with Flask that uses TinyMCE for rich text editing. How do I safely store and display user-submitted blog posts?

I'll show you why improper HTML storage leads to stored XSS, then the secure implementation. **VULNERABLE CODE - DO NOT USE:** ```python from flask import Flask, request, render_template_string, session from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy from datetime import datetime app = Flask(__name__) app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///blog.db' db = SQLAlchemy(app) class BlogPost(db.Model): id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True) title = db.Column(db.String(200), nullable=False) content = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False) # DANGER: Raw HTML storage author_id = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False) created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow) @app.route('/post/create', methods=['POST']) def create_post(): title = request.form.get('title') content = request.form.get('content') # From TinyMCE editor # DANGER: Storing raw HTML without sanitization post = BlogPost( title=title, content=content, # Malicious HTML stored as-is author_id=session.get('user_id') ) db.session.add(post) db.session.commit() return redirect(f'/post/{post.id}') @app.route('/post/') def view_post(post_id): post = BlogPost.query.get_or_404(post_id) # DANGER: Rendering raw HTML from database html = ( "" "" f"

{{post.title}}

" f"
{{post.content}}
" "" "" ) return html ``` **Why This Is Dangerous:** An attacker creates a blog post with malicious content: **Attack Payload:** ```html

Check out this great article!

``` When ANY user views this blog post, their session is stolen. Unlike reflected XSS, this persists in the database and affects every visitor. **Real-World Impact:** { "real_world_incident": "WordPress plugin stored XSS vulnerabilities affected 4M+ sites in 2023, attackers injected malicious scripts in blog comments and user profiles", "impact": "4 million websites compromised, session hijacking, admin account takeover, malware distribution", "attack_vector": "Stored XSS in rich text editor content bypassing HTML sanitization", "cve": "CVE-2023-2732", "year": 2023 } **SECURE IMPLEMENTATION:** ```python from flask import Flask, request, render_template, session, abort from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy import bleach from bleach.css_sanitizer import CSSSanitizer from datetime import datetime import html app = Flask(__name__) app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///blog.db' app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'your-secret-key' db = SQLAlchemy(app) class BlogPost(db.Model): id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True) title = db.Column(db.String(200), nullable=False) content = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False) content_sanitized = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False) # Store sanitized version author_id = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False) created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow) updated_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, onupdate=datetime.utcnow) class BlogPostSanitizer: def __init__(self): # Strict allowlist for blog content self.ALLOWED_TAGS = [ 'p', 'br', 'strong', 'em', 'u', 'h1', 'h2', 'h3', 'h4', 'h5', 'h6', 'blockquote', 'code', 'pre', 'ul', 'ol', 'li', 'a', 'img' ] self.ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTES = { 'a': ['href', 'title', 'rel'], 'img': ['src', 'alt', 'title', 'width', 'height'], 'code': ['class'], # For syntax highlighting } # CSS sanitizer for safe style attributes self.css_sanitizer = CSSSanitizer( allowed_css_properties=['color', 'background-color', 'font-size', 'text-align'] ) self.ALLOWED_PROTOCOLS = ['http', 'https', 'mailto'] def sanitize_content(self, html_content): ''' Sanitize HTML content using bleach with strict rules. ''' if not html_content: return '' # Step 1: Basic sanitization clean_html = bleach.clean( html_content, tags=self.ALLOWED_TAGS, attributes=self.ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTES, protocols=self.ALLOWED_PROTOCOLS, strip=True # Remove disallowed tags completely ) # Step 2: Link safety - add rel="noopener noreferrer" to all links clean_html = bleach.linkify( clean_html, callbacks=[self.set_link_attributes], skip_tags=['pre', 'code'] ) return clean_html def set_link_attributes(self, attrs, new=False): '''Add security attributes to links.''' # Always set rel to prevent window.opener access attrs[(None, 'rel')] = 'noopener noreferrer' # Validate href href = attrs.get((None, 'href'), '') if href and not any(href.startswith(p + ':') for p in self.ALLOWED_PROTOCOLS): # Dangerous protocol - remove href attrs[(None, 'href')] = '#' return attrs def sanitize_title(self, title): '''Sanitize title (no HTML allowed).''' if not title: return '' # Escape all HTML return html.escape(title.strip()[:200]) # Global sanitizer instance sanitizer = BlogPostSanitizer() @app.route('/post/create', methods=['POST']) def create_post(): if 'user_id' not in session: abort(401) title = request.form.get('title', '') content = request.form.get('content', '') # Validate input if not title or not content: return "Title and content required", 400 if len(title) > 200: return "Title too long", 400 if len(content) > 50000: # Reasonable limit return "Content too long", 400 # Sanitize content clean_title = sanitizer.sanitize_title(title) clean_content = sanitizer.sanitize_content(content) # Store both original and sanitized (for potential re-editing) post = BlogPost( title=clean_title, content=content, # Original (for re-editing) content_sanitized=clean_content, # Display this author_id=session['user_id'] ) db.session.add(post) db.session.commit() return redirect(f'/post/{post.id}') @app.route('/post/') def view_post(post_id): post = BlogPost.query.get_or_404(post_id) # Use template rendering with auto-escaping # Pass sanitized content marked as safe return render_template('post.html', post=post) ``` **Template (post.html) with CSP:** ```html {{ post.title }}

{{ post.title }}

Posted on {{ post.created_at.strftime('%Y-%m-%d') }}
{{ post.content_sanitized | safe }}
``` **Key Security Controls:** 1. **bleach Library**: Industry-standard HTML sanitizer for Python 2. **Tag Allowlist**: Only safe formatting tags permitted 3. **Attribute Allowlist**: Prevents event handlers (onclick, onerror, etc.) 4. **Protocol Allowlist**: Blocks javascript: and data: URLs 5. **Link Safety**: Automatic rel="noopener noreferrer" on all links 6. **CSP Header**: Blocks inline scripts as final defense layer 7. **Double Storage**: Keep original for re-editing, sanitized for display 8. **Input Validation**: Length limits prevent abuse The malicious payload is neutralized: `` tags with `onerror` handlers are stripped entirely by bleach.
What if I need to support embedded videos and syntax highlighting in code blocks? How do I handle that safely?

For advanced content like embedded videos and syntax highlighting, you need additional security layers:

```python
import bleach
from bleach.css_sanitizer import CSSSanitizer
import re
from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs

class AdvancedBlogPostSanitizer:
    def __init__(self):
        # Extended tag allowlist
        self.ALLOWED_TAGS = [
            'p', 'br', 'strong', 'em', 'u', 'h1', 'h2', 'h3', 'h4', 'h5', 'h6',
            'blockquote', 'code', 'pre', 'ul', 'ol', 'li', 'a', 'img',
            'div', 'span', 'iframe'  # For embeds and syntax highlighting
        ]

        self.ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTES = {
            'a': ['href', 'title', 'rel'],
            'img': ['src', 'alt', 'title', 'width', 'height'],
            'code': ['class'],
            'pre': ['class'],
            'div': ['class'],
            'span': ['class'],
            'iframe': ['src', 'width', 'height', 'frameborder', 'allowfullscreen'],
        }

        # Trusted video providers
        self.ALLOWED_IFRAME_DOMAINS = [
            'www.youtube.com',
            'www.youtube-nocookie.com',
            'player.vimeo.com',
            'www.dailymotion.com'
        ]

        # Syntax highlighting class allowlist
        self.ALLOWED_CODE_CLASSES = re.compile(r'^language-[a-z0-9]+$')

    def sanitize_content(self, html_content):
        '''Sanitize with support for embeds and code highlighting.'''
        if not html_content:
            return ''

        # Step 1: Pre-process iframes
        html_content = self.sanitize_iframes(html_content)

        # Step 2: Bleach sanitization
        clean_html = bleach.clean(
            html_content,
            tags=self.ALLOWED_TAGS,
            attributes=self.validate_attributes,
            protocols=['http', 'https'],
            strip=True
        )

        # Step 3: Post-process for additional safety
        clean_html = self.post_process_html(clean_html)

        return clean_html

    def sanitize_iframes(self, html_content):
        '''
        Validate and sanitize iframe embeds.
        Only allow trusted video providers.
        '''
        from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

        soup = BeautifulSoup(html_content, 'html.parser')

        for iframe in soup.find_all('iframe'):
            src = iframe.get('src', '')

            if not self.is_safe_iframe_src(src):
                # Remove unsafe iframe
                iframe.decompose()
            else:
                # Add security attributes
                iframe['sandbox'] = 'allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-presentation'
                # Remove any JavaScript: or data: attributes
                for attr in list(iframe.attrs.keys()):
                    if attr.lower() not in ['src', 'width', 'height', 'frameborder', 'allowfullscreen', 'sandbox']:
                        del iframe[attr]

        return str(soup)

    def is_safe_iframe_src(self, src):
        '''Check if iframe src is from trusted domain.'''
        try:
            parsed = urlparse(src)

            # Must be HTTPS
            if parsed.scheme != 'https':
                return False

            # Check domain allowlist
            if parsed.netloc not in self.ALLOWED_IFRAME_DOMAINS:
                return False

            # Additional validation for YouTube
            if 'youtube' in parsed.netloc:
                # Ensure it's an embed URL, not a regular video page
                if not parsed.path.startswith('/embed/'):
                    return False

            return True
        except:
            return False

    def validate_attributes(self, tag, name, value):
        '''Custom attribute validation.'''
        # Check if attribute is allowed for this tag
        if tag not in self.ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTES:
            return False

        if name not in self.ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTES[tag]:
            return False

        # Special validation for class attributes (syntax highlighting)
        if name == 'class':
            if tag in ['code', 'pre']:
                # Only allow language-* classes for syntax highlighting
                if not self.ALLOWED_CODE_CLASSES.match(value):
                    return False
            elif tag in ['div', 'span']:
                # Very limited classes for containers
                if value not in ['code-block', 'highlight', 'syntax']:
                    return False

        # Special validation for href/src
        if name in ['href', 'src']:
            try:
                parsed = urlparse(value)
                # Block javascript:, data:, vbscript: protocols
                if parsed.scheme in ['javascript', 'data', 'vbscript', 'file']:
                    return False
            except:
                return False

        return True

    def post_process_html(self, html):
        '''Additional safety checks after bleach.'''
        from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

        soup = BeautifulSoup(html, 'html.parser')

        # Ensure all links have security attributes
        for link in soup.find_all('a'):
            link['rel'] = 'noopener noreferrer'
            if link.get('href', '').startswith('http'):
                link['target'] = '_blank'

        # Validate all images
        for img in soup.find_all('img'):
            src = img.get('src', '')
            if not src.startswith(('https://', 'http://', '/')):
                img.decompose()

        return str(soup)

# Flask route with versioning
@app.route('/post/create', methods=['POST'])
def create_post_v2():
    if 'user_id' not in session:
        abort(401)

    title = request.form.get('title', '')
    content = request.form.get('content', '')

    # Input validation
    if not title or not content:
        return "Title and content required", 400

    if len(content) > 100000:  # Higher limit for embedded content
        return "Content too long", 400

    # Advanced sanitization
    sanitizer = AdvancedBlogPostSanitizer()
    clean_title = html.escape(title.strip()[:200])
    clean_content = sanitizer.sanitize_content(content)

    # Content versioning - store history
    post = BlogPost(
        title=clean_title,
        content=content,  # Original for editing
        content_sanitized=clean_content,
        author_id=session['user_id'],
        content_version=1
    )

    db.session.add(post)
    db.session.commit()

    return redirect(f'/post/{post.id}')
```

**Security Features:**

1. **iframe Domain Allowlist**: Only trusted video providers (YouTube, Vimeo)
2. **HTTPS Enforcement**: All embeds must use HTTPS
3. **iframe Sandboxing**: Restricts iframe capabilities
4. **Syntax Highlighting Classes**: Only `language-*` pattern allowed
5. **Protocol Blocking**: Prevents javascript:, data:, vbscript: URLs
6. **Link Security**: Automatic noopener/noreferrer on all external links
7. **Image Validation**: Only HTTP/HTTPS images allowed
8. **Content Versioning**: Track changes for security audits

This allows rich content (videos, syntax highlighting) while maintaining strong XSS protection through multiple validation layers.

**Operational Security Considerations:**

- **Logging & Monitoring**: Log all security-relevant events (authentication attempts, authorization failures, suspicious patterns) to a centralized logging system for detection and forensic analysis.
- **Detection**: Implement alerts for anomalous behavior such as repeated failed attempts, unusual access patterns, or privilege escalation attempts.
- **Least Privilege**: Ensure services and users operate with minimum necessary permissions to limit blast radius of potential compromises.
- **Defense-in-Depth**: Layer multiple security controls (input validation, parameterized queries, WAF, rate limiting, network segmentation) so that failure of one control doesn't lead to full compromise.
